summary: Phone is the weakest automatable OSINT category. Validation/format/region is fully solvable for free and OFFLINE via Google's libphonenumber (python `phonenumbers`) — no key, no network. Carrier and line-type are reliably solvable only via paid HLR-backed APIs (Twilio Lookup is the production backbone); the offline `phonenumbers` carrier guess is best-effort/inaccurate, and free tiers (Numverify/Veriphone) give carrier+line_type at low monthly quotas. REVERSE lookup -> owner name and spam/fraud reputation are PAID-DATA-ONLY (Trestle, Twilio caller_name, IPQualityScore). PhoneInfoga (already wired) is officially 'stable but unmaintained' and only does thin local parsing + footprint generation; do not build a roadmap on it.

best.name: python-phonenumbers (Google libphonenumber port)
best.maintained: active (v9.0.18, 2026; David Drysdale tracks Google upstream metadata)
best.notes: OFFLINE — no key, no network, no quota. The correct default for valid/format/region/E.164 normalization. number_type() distinguishes mobile/fixed-line/VoIP/toll-free FROM THE NUMBERING PLAN (not live HLR) so it is structural, not a carrier query. Must pass region=None only if number is E.164 (has +); otherwise supply a default region. Selector should be normalized to E.164 before any downstream paid lookup.

runner_up.install: API key from https://veriphone.io (free tier 1,000 validations/mo)
runner_up.notes: Hosted alternative if offline lib is undesirable. Free tier = format validation + line-type + region (1,000/mo); carrier/HLR is paid-tier only. JSON out. Good cross-check but libphonenumber covers the free part without a key.

capabilities.gaps: None significant — offline validation is fully solved for free. Only caveat: number_type() reflects the numbering plan, not number portability, so a ported mobile may misreport. Live line-type needs an HLR API (see carrier capability).

best.name: Twilio Lookup v2 (Line Type Intelligence / SIM Swap)
best.install: Twilio account + Account SID & Auth Token; no SDK needed (plain REST + basic auth)
best.notes: Production backbone. Base validation/formatting is FREE; premium data packages billed per request: line_type_intelligence ~$0.008, caller_name ~$0.01, sim_swap, call_forwarding, line_status, identity_match ~$0.10, sms_pumping_risk. line_type_intelligence returns mobile/landline/fixed-VoIP/non-fixed-VoIP/toll-free + carrier name + mobile_country_code/mobile_network_code. SIM-swap = last SIM change recency (fraud signal). Wrap with credential vault.

runner_up.install: Free API key from https://numverify.com (free tier 100 requests/mo)
runner_up.notes: apilayer; free tier carrier + location + line_type (mobile/landline/VoIP) for 232 countries, but only 100 req/mo and HTTPS is paid-tier on the legacy plan. JSON. Good free spot-check for carrier when Twilio budget is unavailable; not for volume. PhoneInfoga's numverify scanner uses this same key.

runner_up.run: python -c "import phonenumbers; from phonenumbers import carrier; n=phonenumbers.parse('{selector}',None); print(carrier.name_for_number(n,'en'))"
runner_up.notes: OFFLINE carrier GUESS from the original-assignment metadata only. Best-effort, frequently wrong for ported numbers and US numbers. Use as a free hint, never as confirmed carrier.

capabilities.gaps: Reliable live carrier + line-type requires a paid HLR-backed API (Twilio/Numverify-paid/Veriphone-paid). No free tool gives accurate carrier at volume. SIM-swap and call-forwarding signals are Twilio-only (paid) with no free equivalent.

best.name: Trestle Reverse Phone API
best.install: API key from https://trestleiq.com (free trial, no credit card); pay-as-you-go ~$0.07/query
best.notes: Best agent-callable phone->owner-name source. Returns owner name + demographics, current/historical addresses, and associated people. US-centric. Free TRIAL exists (good for a real investigation), then paid. This is the realistic 'last mile' to identity.

runner_up.install: Twilio account; same endpoint as Lookup v2
runner_up.notes: CNAM caller-name (~$0.01/req). US-only, landline-biased, often blank for mobiles. Cheaper than Trestle but much thinner attribution. identity_match (~$0.10) verifies a name you already have rather than discovering one.

capabilities.gaps: PAID-DATA-ONLY across the board. No free/open tool maps a phone to an owner name reliably; the audit confirms name-attribution is paid-data-only (Trestle, Pipl, OSINT Industries, Predicta). Free CLIs (PhoneInfoga/Phunter) only generate Google-dork footprints, they do not return owner identity.

best.name: IPQualityScore Phone Validation API
best.install: Free API key from https://www.ipqualityscore.com (free plan ~1,000 lookups to start)
best.notes: Best fraud/spam-risk signal with a usable free allocation. Returns fraud_score (>=75 suspicious, >=85 risky, >=90 high-risk), active status, carrier, line_type, country, recent_abuse, VOIP/prepaid/risky flags, leaked flag. ~150 countries. Pay-as-you-go ~$0.0005/lookup beyond free. Single GET, clean JSON — easy to wire.

runner_up.notes: Narrow: SMS-pumping / artificial-traffic fraud risk only (not general spam-caller reputation). Paid. Complements IPQS for SMS-fraud-specific scoring.

capabilities.gaps: True crowd-sourced spam-CALLER reputation (Truecaller/Hiya/Robokiller style) has NO official agent-callable free API — Truecaller's API is enterprise/gated and ToS-restricted for scraping. IPQS gives an algorithmic fraud score, not community spam reports. This is a real coverage gap.

excluded.reason: ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in src/tools/phone_tools.py (PhoneInfogaTool, status=already_implemented). Officially 'stable but unmaintained' (own README, last release Feb 2024; repo may be archived anytime). Does thin LOCAL parsing (libphonenumber-equivalent) + Google-dork footprint generation; its numverify/google scanners need keys. Keep as-is but do not invest further.
Unmaintained hobby CLI; no native structured/JSON output; wraps the same libphonenumber + footprint generation we get directly from phonenumbers. Redundant.
Abandoned hobby CLI, no JSON, no maintained releases. Redundant with phonenumbers.
spider863644/PhoneNumber-OSINT — small unvetted research CLI, no structured output, automatable_now=false in buckets.
Phunter CLI only generates link/footprint guesses (Truecaller/WhatsApp/social URLs to check manually); returns no confirmed structured attribution. Manual-research aid, not a structured collector.
Duplicate catalog entry for PhoneInfoga (already implemented; see above).
Moriarty Project — unmaintained, scrapes social/spam sites with no stable structured output; brittle and ToS-risky.

bellingcat/telegram-phone-number-checker is a TELEGRAM-account-existence tool (belongs to telegram bucket, not phone validation/carrier/owner/reputation). Requires Telegram API credentials + session; stateful, out of scope here.
Best-known spam-caller DB, but NO official free agent-callable API; enterprise-gated and ToS-prohibits scraping. Manual/paid-only — cannot wire as a structured collector.
Viable free-tier validation API but fully redundant with the offline phonenumbers lib (validation) and IPQS/Numverify (carrier/reputation); no unique capability to justify another key.
